prediction_test_verdicts.reason: The packet gives full KWIC-filtered exact-query counts of 71 plural target rows versus 1 singular target row, with most raw singular hits removed as false positives.
The 1:1 filtered target count contrasts with animate bunch but is too sparse to estimate an inanimate profile.
The majority cells provide 105 filtered plural target rows and 0 singular target rows, though the minority half of the tranche contributes no evidence.
The denominator audit strengthens rather than reverses the exact-query result by adding 37 omitted plural opportunities and 0 omitted singular opportunities.
The direction is strongly supportive, but the largest positive lots cell is sample-coded rather than fully filtered, so the strict pass condition is only partly met.
Both registered minority strings are raw zero, so the packet supplies no target rows for a minority-family inference.

card_result_verdicts.reason: The module survives within the narrow corpus-production scope because the strongest checked cells defeat a surface-head-number baseline, while the packet explicitly blocks broader grammaticality or numeric-score claims.

strongest_instability: The partitive/QN verdict is the main overstatement because one high-count positive cell is only a bounded sample despite a pass condition requiring enough KWIC filtering.
minimal_change: Downgrade the partitive/QN prediction-test label from passed to mixed while retaining the card-level survives_as_module result and no-score-change decision.
